notes: Two concurrent agy CLI sessions launched in the SAME cwd (driver start_session step launches a second agy as a separate conversation/transcript). Antigravity is a single-birth adapter: each conversation gets a stable session_id from its <conv-id> transcript dir at launch (no presession proc-<PID> reconcile), so phases anchor on working transitions and on same_session_as/new_session. Asserts: (1) session 1 does an independent working->ready turn; (2) a DISTINCT session appears (s2_birth new_session:true) and does its own working->ready turn; (3) session 1 is driven AGAIN while session 2 is alive (s1_turn2_*, pinned same_session_as s1_turn1_start), proving the two transcript-first conv-id sessions stay DISTINCT and never merge — the second turn lands on session 1's own <conv-id>, not session 2's. Transcript-first: a conv-id session never binds a PID (pid=0), so distinctness is structural (unique <conv-id> dirs), NOT PID-disambiguation. No token/cost observations: Antigravity omits usage from the JSONL — only model/agent are asserted, and model is left provisional for record to fill.

text: Session 1 first prompt drives ready -> working (anchors session 1's <conv-id>)
Session 1 first turn completes: working -> ready (terminal PLANNER_RESPONSE with no tool_calls)
Session 2 starts in the SAME cwd as a DISTINCT session_id (a second agy conversation) and appears in ready (new_session proves it is not session 1)
Session 2 first prompt drives ready -> working — independent of session 1
Session 2 first turn completes: working -> ready

Session 1 is driven AGAIN while session 2 is still alive — a working transition on session 1's original <conv-id> proves the two transcript-first sessions stay distinct; the turn lands on session 1's own conv-id, not session 2's (no merge)
Session 1 second turn completes: working -> ready
